notes: v289 — Several upgrades in one update. NEW — ⛶ FULL SCREEN: a button in the top bar expands the app to fill your whole screen; press Esc (or tap the button again) to come back. FIX — in light mode, the loaded-market status pill (‘ASX · date · N stocks · live’) was staying dark; it now switches to a light style like the rest of the app. FIX — the 🔔 'What's changed for me' briefing could reopen by itself every few seconds after you closed it; it now opens at most once per fresh data day and stays closed when you dismiss it (tap the 🔔 button any time to reopen it yourself). NEW — ☀️ LIGHT & DARK MODE: a sun/moon button in the top bar switches the whole app between the dark theme and a new light theme — easier on the eyes in daylight or if you simply prefer a bright screen. It remembers your choice on each device, and the very first time you open it, it quietly matches whatever light-or-dark setting your phone or computer already uses. The accents (gold, green, red) keep their identity, just deepened a touch so everything stays crisp and readable on white. NEW — 📋 MY TRADING RULES: a place to write your own plan and have the app help you keep it. Set your take-profit %, stop-loss %, the most you'll put in any one share, and how many positions you'll hold at once — plus a free-text checklist of your own rules ('wait for a green day', 'never average down', 'review every Sunday'). Your 🎯 target and 🛑 stop then auto-fill every practice buy, and if you go over your max size, hold more positions than your rule allows, or drift past your stop, your 🔔 briefing flags it gently — never a block. It's your plan, in your words: a discipline aid, clearly not advice. SECURITY — a routine re-audit of everything drawn on screen (after this batch of new reports and the briefing) found one spot to tighten: confirmed-announcement headlines were cleaned of any hidden markup when freshly fetched, but not when re-read from their saved cache. That's now cleaned the moment it enters the app, everywhere, no matter the source — a purely protective change with no effect on normal use. FIX — a leftover ticker search no longer follows you around: searching a share and then opening ⭐ My Watchlist used to silently filter the list (e.g. '1 in My Watchlist' when 27 are starred). Every way into the watchlist — the ⭐ button, the ⚙ Lists panel, switching lists, or tapping a list in the 🔔 briefing — now clears the search box first — the full list always shows. Running any report already cleared it. NEW REPORT — 🕳 GAP REPORT: shares that OPENED 3%+ above or below yesterday's close (overnight news or a rush of morning orders), split into gapped & HELD (buyers stayed all day — strength) vs gapped & FADED (the jump fizzled — a common trap), with a tap-through summary panel grouping every gap by what happened next. It uses the daily opens the app now stores (part of the candles fix) — if most of your shares don't have opens saved yet, one 🔄 Refresh/redownload fills them in and the report says so honestly. NEW REPORT — 🧱 STRONG TRENDS: the strongest up-trend structure there is — price above the 20-day average, above the 50-day, above the 200-day (the full 'stack') — ranked by trend quality: steady slope, not overstretched, quiet accumulation. If your shares don't have 200 days of stored history yet, it tells you exactly that instead of just showing nothing. STICKY LIQUIDITY FLOOR — your 💧 Liquidity filter (Type tab) now SURVIVES every scan instead of being cleared: set '≥$250k/day' once and Smart Money, Top 20, Gap Report and every other scan quietly skip the un-tradeable shares (the 💧 badge stays visible so you always know it's on; Clear all still resets it). CLEARER BUTTONS — 🚨 Sharp Mover now says what it really is (the SIZE of today's move vs that share's own normal day) and 🔍 Unusual Activity too (five suspicious PATTERNS in HOW it traded) — two different questions, now impossible to confuse. TIDIER — the More-scans overlay retired the 📈 Momentum and ⚡ Volume Spike presets (the 🎯 Smart Money and 🔥 Volume Surge buttons do the same job better) and the remaining presets are relabelled with a plain-English explainer. 🔔 BRIEFING — What's changed for me shows your watchlists by NAME: holdings first, then each list with how many flagged today ('2 of 8 flagged'), an explicit 'all quiet today' when none did, and tap a list's name to open it. It checks YOUR practice holdings and watchlists each time fresh data loads — targets and stops hit or near, big moves, confirmed price-sensitive news, volume surges and trend crosses — and opens by itself once per new data day, only when there's something to say. CANDLES FIXED — candlestick view finally works: the daily refresh had been quietly wiping the open/high/low that candles need. Stored history is now preserved and grows daily; 🔄 Refresh/redownload restores full-depth candles immediately, and until then the chart draws clearly-labelled approximate candles instead of refusing. Candles work in demo mode too. FIX — the portfolio's 'return % is not trustworthy' warning behaves: tiny rounding residue no longer warns, bigger differences show the exact $ amount with a one-tap '✓ This balance is right — stop warning' that sticks, and it only returns if something NEW drifts. REPORT CARD — every flag's fires split into 📢 news-driven vs 🔇 quiet, plus how many WINNERS had news behind them; fires the app's announcement log can't cover are marked 'unknown', never guessed. CHARTS — professional refresh: round-number gridlines on the right axis, month ticks, dotted last-price line, soft glow, live price readout in the header. FILTERS — 💧 Liquidity ($ traded/day: Any / ≥$50k / ≥$250k / ≥$1M) replaces the old market-cap filter, and an RSI zone filter (Oversold ≤30 / Neutral / Overbought ≥70). 🪜 PULLBACK IN UPTREND report: uptrend shares that pulled back to the 20-day line with RSI cooled to 35–55. The 🎓 Lessons and 📖 Help cover all of this, including the two new reports. PAPER TRADING — the ↩ undo button removed from trade history (closed trades stay honest). DEMO — practice shares carry their correct real-world names, and demo data can't fake a gap. All figures are end-of-day estimates, not advice.
